dict.string:
Zone 5 Logger — auto-logs Zone 5 heart-rate sessions from Apple Watch to a GitHub CSV.
WHAT IT DOES: 1. Reads your peak heart rate from Health. 2. If it crosses your Zone 5 threshold, builds a CSV row (date, "Zone5", peak HR). 3. Base64-encodes it and PUTs it to a GitHub-hosted CSV via the GitHub API. 4. Notifies you it logged.
Based on Helgerud 2007 (HIIT is highly effective for VO₂max). No Calculate actions — threshold comparison via a conditional.
⚠️ PRIVACY NOTE (why this scores yellow): it sends your heart-rate data to a remote server (GitHub) and base64-encodes the payload. That's legitimate here — it's YOUR repo and YOUR token — but it is exactly the kind of off-device data flow the safety scanner flags so you can see it. Put your GitHub token in the "Get Contents of URL" action's headers.
CUSTOMIZE: your Zone 5 threshold BPM, and the GitHub repo/file + token.
